citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
There was 1 guinea pig observed with a health condition requiring treatment in accordance with the Program of Veterinary
Care or consultation with the attending veterinarian. A brown and white adult guinea pig in Pen 111 had a lump under the
chin approximately 2cm in size where the hair was missing and there was an open area of skin that was red in color. The
animal was alert and appeared in good body condition. The facility had not observed the health condition or administered
any treatments. The condition was addressed in the Program of Veterinary Care and the licensee moved the animal to a
sick pen to begin treatment.
Failure to observe health conditions in the animals can delay treatment and lead to a worsening of the condition.
The facility must perform adequate daily observations to assess the health and well-being of the animals.
Correct by: January 29, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

Failure to observe health conditions in the animals can delay treatment and lead to a worsening of the condition. 
The facility must perform adequate daily observations to assess the health and well-being of the animals.

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
